emu: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Eastern Muria.
eon: noun. Eternity, the duration of the universe.
mem: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Mangala.
memo: noun. A short note; a memorandum.
men: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Mende (Sierra Leone).
menu: noun. The details of the food to be served at a banquet; a bill of fare.
met: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Mato.
mom: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Monimbo.
mome: noun. A dull or stupid person.
moment: noun. A particular point, or relatively small region.
momentum: noun. Of a body in motion: the tendency of a body to maintain its inertial motion; the product of its mass and velocity, or the vector sum
mon: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Mongolian.
monte: noun. A game in which three or four cards are dealt face - up and players bet on which of them will first be matched in suit by others
mot: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Barí.
mote: noun. A small particle; a speck.
moue: noun. A pout, especially as expressing mock-annoyance or flirtatiousness.
mum: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Maiwala.
mumm: verb. Archaic form of mum (“to act in pantomime or dumb show”).
mun: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-5 language code for Munda languages.
muon: noun. An unstable elementary particle in the lepton family, having similar properties to the electron but with a mass 207 times greater.
mut: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Western Muria.
mute: adjective. Not having the power of speech; dumb.
muton: noun. A unit of mutation forming part of a recon.
net: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nete.
nom: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nocamán.
nome: noun. A prefecture or unit of regional government in Greece.
not: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nomatsiguenga.
notum: noun. The back; the dorsal side of the thorax in insects or nudibranches.
nut: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nùng.
omen: noun. Something which portends or is perceived to portend either a good or evil event or circumstance in the future, or which causes a
omentum: noun. Either of two folds of the peritoneum that support the viscera.
one: noun. NATO & ICAO radiotelephony clear code (spelling-alphabet name) for the digit 1.
out: adverb. Away from the inside or centre.
ten: noun. NATO, ICAO, ITU & IMO radiotelephony code for 10, used only with o'clock to indicate direction.
toe: symbol. Former ISO 639-3 language code for Tomedes.
tom: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tombulu.
tome: noun. One in a series of volumes.
ton: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Tongan.
tun: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tunica.
umm: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Umon.
unto: conjunction. Up to the time or degree that; until.
